mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly job gains (PAYEMS > 150k) indicate robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 150k, or the signal's IC drops below 0.05 in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO > 100.5) signals stronger manufacturing activity, which correlates with improved corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in 50%+ of cases where INDPRO exceeds 100.5, or the signal's IC falls below 0.04 in 5-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing starts (HOUST > 1400k) reflect heightened consumer confidence and demand for durable goods, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in 55%+ of instances where HOUST exceeds 1.4M, or the signal's IC drops below 0.03 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5pp+ increase in unemployment rate (UNRATE) over 3 months signals deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in 60%+ of cases where UNRATE rises by 0.5pp+, or the signal's IC falls below -0.04 in 5-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL indicate tightening monetary policy amid inflation, which raises discount rates and depresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in 55%+ of cases where both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL rise by 0.1pp/0.2pp, or the interaction effect's IC drops below -0.03 in 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising FEDFUNDS correlates with higher risk-free rates that reduce equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in 50%+ of tightening cycles, or the signal's IC falls below -0.05 in 5-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation (CPIAUCSL > 262) signals central bank inaction, which erodes real returns and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in 55%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 262, or the signal's IC drops below -0.04 in 3-year rolling windows.
